questions.q: King Bhagiratha is most famous in Hindu tradition for bringing which sacred river down to the earth?
questions.explanation: Bhagiratha is celebrated as the king who brought the celestial river Ganga down to the earth.

questions.q: To which famous royal dynasty did King Bhagiratha belong?
questions.explanation: Bhagiratha was a king of the Ikshvaku, or Solar, dynasty of Ayodhya.

questions.q: According to the Ramayana, why did Bhagiratha undertake great austerities to bring the Ganga to earth?
options: To liberate the ashes of his ancestors
questions.explanation: Bhagiratha sought to bring the Ganga down so its waters could liberate the ashes of his sixty thousand ancestors.

questions.q: Which deity caught the mighty Ganga in his matted locks as she descended, according to the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: According to the Ramayana, Shiva caught the descending Ganga in his matted hair to spare the earth from her force.

questions.q: How many ancestors of Bhagiratha, the sons of Sagara, awaited liberation by the Ganga's waters?
questions.explanation: The Ramayana relates that Sagara's sixty thousand sons had been reduced to ashes and awaited liberation.

questions.q: In which major epic is the story of Bhagiratha bringing the Ganga to earth famously told?
questions.explanation: The descent of the Ganga through Bhagiratha's efforts is narrated in the Ramayana.

questions.q: Because of Bhagiratha's efforts, the river Ganga is given which well-known epithet in the tradition?
questions.explanation: In honour of Bhagiratha, one of the Ganga's most celebrated names is Bhagirathi.

questions.q: What kind of effort did Bhagiratha chiefly perform to accomplish his sacred goal of bringing down the Ganga?
questions.explanation: Bhagiratha performed long and severe austerities (tapas) to win the boon of the Ganga's descent.

questions.q: In which city of the Ikshvaku kings is Bhagiratha traditionally placed as a ruler?
questions.explanation: Bhagiratha belonged to the Ikshvaku line of Ayodhya, the same dynasty as Rama.

questions.q: The descent of the Ganga is remembered as Bhagiratha's enduring contribution because it brought what to his ancestors?
questions.explanation: The Ganga's sacred waters granted liberation to the ashes of Bhagiratha's ancestors.

questions.q: Whose sons were the sixty thousand ancestors that Bhagiratha sought to liberate?
questions.explanation: The sixty thousand were the sons of King Sagara, an earlier ancestor of Bhagiratha in the Ikshvaku line.

questions.q: According to the Ramayana, how were Sagara's sixty thousand sons reduced to ashes in the first place?
options: By the wrath of the sage Kapila
questions.explanation: The sons were burnt to ashes by the fiery glance of the sage Kapila when they wrongly accused him of stealing their sacrificial horse.

questions.q: Why was Shiva asked to receive the Ganga upon her descent, according to the Ramayana?
options: To divide her into seven streams himself
To break the force of her fall so the earth would not be shattered
To carry her to the netherworld
questions.explanation: The earth could not bear the Ganga's tremendous fall, so Shiva received her in his matted locks to soften the descent.

questions.q: From which divine realm did the Ganga descend to the earth in the account of Bhagiratha's penance?
questions.explanation: The Ganga was a celestial river of the heavens before Bhagiratha's austerities brought her down to the earth.

questions.q: After being released from Shiva's locks, the Ganga is said to have followed Bhagiratha to reach the ashes of his ancestors located where?
questions.explanation: The Ganga flowed down and finally into Patala (the netherworld) to touch the ashes of Sagara's sons and liberate them.

questions.q: As the Ganga followed Bhagiratha's chariot, whose sacrificial enclosure did she flood, leading to a famous further episode?
questions.explanation: The Ganga flooded the sacrificial ground of the sage Jahnu, who in anger drank her up.

questions.q: Because the sage Jahnu released the Ganga after absorbing her, the river earned which additional name?
questions.explanation: Released from the sage Jahnu, the Ganga came to be called Jahnavi, the daughter of Jahnu.

questions.q: Bhagiratha is described as belonging to the same royal line as which hero of the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Bhagiratha was of the Ikshvaku Solar dynasty, the same lineage into which Rama was later born.

questions.q: In the Ramayana's account, what did Bhagiratha do to guide the released Ganga toward his ancestors' ashes?
options: He led her by riding ahead in his chariot
He carried her in a pot
He dug a great canal by hand
questions.explanation: Bhagiratha went ahead in his chariot, and the Ganga followed the path he led toward the netherworld.

questions.q: The phrase 'Bhagiratha prayatna' in Indian tradition has come to mean what kind of effort, after the king's deeds?
questions.explanation: Drawn from Bhagiratha's long perseverance, 'Bhagiratha prayatna' denotes a monumental, unwavering effort.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, the story of Bhagiratha and the Ganga is recounted to Rama by which sage?
questions.explanation: The sage Vishvamitra narrates the descent of the Ganga and Bhagiratha's role to Rama and Lakshmana in the Bala Kanda.

questions.q: Before Bhagiratha succeeded, earlier kings of his line had also attempted to bring the Ganga down. Bhagiratha is thus remembered chiefly for which quality?
options: His persistence in penance across difficulties
questions.explanation: Bhagiratha is honoured for his relentless persistence in austerity that finally accomplished what others could not.

questions.q: Whose sacrificial horse, when it went missing, set in motion the chain of events that led to the sixty thousand sons being burnt to ashes?
questions.explanation: King Sagara's horse for the Ashvamedha (horse sacrifice) was hidden, and his sons' search led to their destruction by Kapila.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, who is said to have taken Sagara's sacrificial horse to the netherworld, causing the sons to dig down and meet Kapila?
questions.explanation: Indra carried off the sacrificial horse to Patala, where the sons found it near the meditating sage Kapila.

questions.q: Bhagiratha was the son of which king of the Ikshvaku line, according to the Ramayana's genealogy?
questions.explanation: In the Ramayana's lineage, Bhagiratha was the son of King Dilipa, who himself had failed to bring down the Ganga.

questions.q: Bhagiratha's grandfather Amshuman and father Dilipa had earlier tried to liberate the ancestors but did not succeed. What did this make Bhagiratha within the lineage?
options: The one who finally fulfilled a multi-generational quest
questions.explanation: Bhagiratha completed a task across several generations, succeeding where Amshuman and Dilipa before him had not.

questions.q: Brahma is said to have granted Bhagiratha a boon for his austerities, but warned that only one deity could bear the Ganga's fall. Which deity was that?
questions.explanation: Brahma directed Bhagiratha to propitiate Shiva, as only Shiva could withstand and receive the Ganga's mighty descent.

questions.q: According to the tradition, the Ganga's source as she emerges to flow toward earth is associated with the toe of which deity in some Vaishnava accounts?
questions.explanation: In Vaishnava tradition the celestial Ganga is said to spring from the foot of Vishnu before her descent, after which Shiva receives her.

questions.q: The sacred confluence where the Ganga reached the sea and the ashes of Sagara's sons, linked to Bhagiratha's story, is traditionally identified with which pilgrimage site?
questions.explanation: The place where the Ganga meets the sea, Ganga Sagara, is traditionally tied to the liberation of Sagara's sons.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, the river Ganga that descended through Bhagiratha is also said to have divided into how many streams in the heavens and on earth in some accounts?
questions.explanation: Several accounts describe the Ganga dividing into seven streams as she spread across the heavens and the earth.
